data.title: Intrum AB (publ) reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Intrum AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:ITJTY): Q3 EBIT decreased to SEK -127 M vs. SEK 50 M in Q3’23 driven by a goodwill impairment (SEK 0.7 bn), partially offset by reduced cost base. 
  • Adjusted EBIT decreased by 5% vs. Q3’23 driven by decreased income across both segments.

data.title: Boston Sci stock falls 4% amid FARAPULSE study update
data.description:
Boston Scientific (NYSE:BSX) shares were down 5% in morning trading Wednesday following news that the company has paused a study for its new FARAPULSE Pulse Field Ablation system due to unforeseen complications.

During the company's Q3 earnings call, Boston Scientific (BSX) Chairman and CEO Michael Mahoney noted that the company was studying FARAPULSE in a “very new patient population” of drug-naïve persistent atrial fibrillation patients.

“As we have neared the end of this enrollment, we have elected to temporarily pause the trial to assess a few unanticipated observations. It is our intention to resume enrollment in the near term,” Mahoney said, according to a transcript of the call.

“Based on the totality of clinical evidence and commercial real-world experience, we remain extremely confident in the unique performance of FARAPULSE," Mahoney added.

Earlier Wednesday, Boston Scientific (BSX) released its Q3 earnings report, beating Street estimates on both the top and bottom lines.

Boston Scientific (BSX) received FDA clearance for the FARAPULSE PFA System for the treatment of atrial fibrillation in January.

data.title: Airlines earnings on the runway: Southwest Airlines, American Airlines Q3 preview
data.description:
Earnings have started to garner steam, and investors will turn their attention to the U.S. airlines sectors, with heavyweight American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) and Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) reporting on October 24, Thursday.

According to data compiled by the Bureau of Transportation Statistics, airfares were up 3.2% M/M in September after rising 3.9% in August. Airfares in the U.S. were up 1.6% in September compared to a year ago on an unadjusted basis, and have now increased in four of the last five months

Airlines have enjoyed a boost thanks to pent-up travel demand. Last week, United Airlines’ (UAL) Q3 profit beat the company’s expectations.

However, Delta Airlines' (DAL) Q4 revenue and EPS guidance fell short of consensus estimates, as the firm was hurt by margin pressures and the CrowdStrike (CRWD) outage impact.

American Airlines to stay in the straight and narrow

American Airlines, which had cut its full year profit guidance, is expected to see "stable revenues with capacity expansion, but TRASM and adjusted CASM suggest low margins in Q3," said SA analyst Dhierin Bechai.

The consensus EPS Estimate is $0.17 (-55.3% Y/Y) and the consensus Revenue Estimate is $13.57B (+0.7% Y/Y).

Despite debt concerns and reduced price targets, American Airlines remains a buy due to its current undervaluation and potential upside, Bechai added.

SA analyst, Albert Anthony, however, believes that strong travel demand will keep the company off the turbulent path.

Citigroup analysts Stephen Trent, Filipe Nielsen, and Jay Singh expect the lower interest rates this fall will not only give a jolt to the stock market but be especially bullish for highly leveraged carriers like JetBlue (JBLU) and American Airlines (AAL), both of which could refinance debt and benefit from lower interest expense.

"American Airlines gets buy rating as continued strong travel demand expected to fuel further revenue growth in the near future, helping earnings," said Anthony, and added that the firm is showing signs of reducing its debt load, which could help reduce interest expenses in the future.

Earlier in the day, the U.S. Transportation Department announced that it was fining American Airlines (AAL) a record $50 million for its treatment of disabled passengers, including failing to provide some with adequate assistance and mishandling wheelchairs.

Over the last 2 years, AAL has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 50% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 2 upward revisions and 11 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 1 upward revision and 11 downward.

Shares in the company were up 5.7% since the start of the year.

Not all clear skies for Southwest Airlines

Southwest Airlines (LUV), which raised its Q3 revenue forecast and authorized a $2.5 billion buyback plan, had made several changes to lift up its performance.

The consensus EPS Estimate is $0.04 (-89.5% Y/Y) and the consensus Revenue Estimate is $6.79B.

The carrier and Elliott Investment Management have also started settlement talks to avoid a proxy fight for control over the carrier’s board.

Elliott had reportedly asked for a special shareholder meeting in December after it amassed a $2B stake in Southwest last month and called for sweeping changes at the airline, including the ouster of CEO Bob Jordan, and the airline had responded by announcing sweeping changes to reinvigorate the company’s performance and appease Elliott.

Earlier in October, Jefferies upgraded the airline stock to a Hold rating from Underperform.

"Southwest Airlines' aggressive expansion into new airports has backfired, leading to unprofitable routes and loss of cost edge, necessitating a scale-back for improved efficiency," said SA analyst Curonian Research.

Elliott Management's 11% stake led to proposed changes like assigned seating and premium options, but these are not innovative and may not ensure growth, said SA analyst Johnathan Woulfe.

"Southwest remains far from pre-pandemic recovery, with competitors like United and Delta showing more significant growth, raising doubts about the effectiveness of Elliott's proposed changes," Woulfe.

Over the last 2 years, LUV has beaten EPS estimates 38% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 50% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 2 upward revisions and 8 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 4 upward revisions and 6 downward.

Shares in the company were up 5.5% since the start of the year.

data.title: Aprea Therapeutics falls on data from APR-1051 study in cancer-associated gene alterations
data.description:
• Aprea Therapeutics (NASDAQ:APRE) shares down about 4% after preliminary safety results from early stage study of single-agent APR-1051 in advanced solid tumors harboring cancer-associated gene alterations.
  • The dose escalation part of the study is currently ongoing and is expected to include up to 39 patients with advanced solid tumors.
  • Preliminary results demonstrated that APR-1051 is safe and well-tolerated with no hematologic toxicity.
  • There were no signs of neutropenia, with white blood cells and neutrophils trending up for both patients during the first treatment cycle.
  • All adverse events recorded were Grade 1 and 2, with one Grade 1 AE (abdominal distention) possibly related to APR-1051.
  • Three patients have been dosed to date with data available on two of these. One had disease progression at 49 days, a second withdrew following 36 days of treatment and dosing is ongoing in the third patient.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Atlanta Fed Business Inflation Expectations remains largely unchanged at 2.2%
data.description:
• Atlanta Fed Business Inflation Expectations: Inflation expectations year-ahead remained relatively unchanged at 2.2 percent, on average.
  • Current economic environment: Sales levels and profit margins "compared to normal" increased. Year-over-year unit cost growth remained relatively unchanged at 2.6 percent, on average.
  • Quarterly question: Firms' unit sales levels (compared to "normal" unit sales levels) rose but still remained approximately 6.8 percent below normal.
  • Press Release

data.title: SSAB AB (publ) reports Q3 results
data.description:
• SSAB AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:SSAAF): Q3 Revenue was SEK 24,373 million
  • Operating result was SEK 1,248 million
  • Earnings per share were SEK 1.05
  • Net cash was SEK 13.3 billion
  • Outlook for the fourth quarter of 2024Compared with the third quarter of 2024, SSAB Special Steels’ shipments are assessed to be somewhat lower during the fourth quarter.
  • The costs of raw materials for SSAB Special Steels are expected to be somewhat lower compared to the prior quarter.
  • Raw material costs for SSAB Europe are expected to be stable, whereas for SSAB Americas the costs are expected to be somewhat higher compared to the prior quarter.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: SAVE, SBUX, MCD, QCOM, and more
data.description:
Stock futures continued to edge lower, exhibiting a cautious move on Wednesday ahead of the most anticipated earnings of an EV maker, Tesla (TSLA).

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Texas Instruments (TXN) shares climbed nearly 4% after the company exceeded Q3 expectations, with revenue showing a 9% sequential improvement. For Q4, the company forecasts earnings between $1.07 and $1.29 per share and revenue between $3.7B and $4B. Analysts, however, had projected earnings of $1.35 per share and $4.08B in revenue, both above the midpoint of Texas Instruments' guidance.
  • Spirit Airlines (SAVE) advanced 29% following reports of early-stage merger talks with Frontier Group (ULCC), potentially as part of Spirit's debt restructuring through bankruptcy. The merger discussions, if successful, could provide operational synergies and financial stability, helping Spirit manage its liabilities. This news comes on the heels of a 53% increase in Spirit's share price on Monday after securing a debt-refinancing extension with its credit card processor.
  • Trump Media & Technology Group (DJT) continued its upward momentum on Wednesday, climbing an additional 5% as investors speculated on former President Donald Trump's increasing chances of winning the upcoming November election, now less than two weeks away. This follows a strong performance on Tuesday, when the stock reached its highest level since July, closing with a nearly 10% gain.

Biggest stock losers

  • Enphase Energy (ENPH) shares tumbled 12% after the company posted disappointing Q3 results, with a 31% Y/Y revenue decline and net income dropping to $45.76M, or $0.33/share, from $113.95M, or $0.80/share, a year ago. For Q4, Enphase provided downbeat guidance, projecting revenue between $360M-$400M, a midpoint of $380M below the consensus of $433.5M. The company expects non-GAAP gross margins between 49%-52% with net IRA benefit and 39%-42% excluding it.
  • Despite increasing its dividend by 7%, Starbucks (SBUX) shares dropped over 4% due to disappointing preliminary fourth-quarter results. Global comparable store sales decreased by 7%, and consolidated net revenue declined 3% to $9.1B, below the $9.4B consensus. Non-GAAP EPS came in at $0.80, missing the $1.03 consensus and down from $1.06 last year. This decline was primarily attributed to weaker performance in North America, particularly in the United States, where comparable store sales declined by 6% due to fewer customer transactions. Additionally, Starbucks faced challenges in China, with comparable store sales declining 14% due to heightened competition and a sluggish economic environment. Given the CEO transition and current business conditions, the company has also suspended its financial guidance for the entire FY2025.
  • McDonald's (MCD) shares fell as much as 6% following a warning issued by the Centers for Disease Control (CDC) regarding an E. coli outbreak in Colorado and Nebraska potentially connected to the restaurant chain's Quarter Pounder. Initial investigations suggest that a portion of the illnesses may be attributed to slivered onions used in the Quarter Pounder, sourced from a specific supplier serving three distribution centers. In response, McDonald's has taken immediate safety measures by removing the affected product from its supply chain and pausing the distribution of all slivered onions in the impacted region. Furthermore, the company has temporarily discontinued the sale of Quarter Pounders in restaurants located in Colorado, Kansas, Utah, Wyoming, and parts of several other states.
  • Qualcomm's (QCOM) shares slipped more than 5% after reports surfaced that Arm Holdings decided to terminate a key license agreement that granted the chipmaker access to Arm's intellectual property for chip design. The British chip designer, a longtime partner and key supplier to Qualcomm, issued a 60-day cancellation notice, intensifying their ongoing legal battle. This move escalates an ongoing legal dispute between the two companies, which began in 2022 when Arm sued Qualcomm for breaching contract terms and trademark infringement after Qualcomm acquired Nuvia, a chip-making startup that was also an Arm licensee. Qualcomm argues that the current license covers Nuvia’s operations, while Arm insists that a renegotiation was required after the acquisition.

More on related stocks:
